quests.how: The biggest single hauls are the beanstalk fusion platforms (the Eastern Hills and Lake Hylia stalks each drop around 200 shells) and Gregal of the Wind Tribe, who hands over 100 shells when you save him. Beyond that, cut grass, smash pots, and open chests throughout Hyrule, since small shell drops are common. Stockpile a big reserve before a gallery session so you can cover the rising per-draw cost without running dry.

quests.name: Borlov's Chest Minigame
quests.oneLine: Carlov's younger brother Borlov runs a pick-a-chest gamble for a Rupee payout.
quests.how: The shop opens after you defeat Mazaal and get the Ocarina of Wind. Pay the 10-Rupee fee and pick one of the chests; you keep whatever Rupees are inside (sometimes more than you paid, sometimes nothing). It's pure luck — and Borlov himself dislikes gambling — so treat it as a fun side stop, not a reliable earner. Both Borlov and Carlov also appear as figurines you can win in the gallery.

quests.name: Fifi and the Free Bottle (Stockwell)
quests.location: Stockwell's Shop, Hyrule Town → Stockwell's house, Lake Hylia
quests.oneLine: Deliver Stockwell's dog food to his dog Fifi and the shopkeeper lets you keep the Bottle for free.
quests.how: When you try to take the Bottle of Dog Food from behind Stockwell's counter, he agrees to let you keep the empty Bottle if you go feed it to his dog, Fifi. Fifi lives in Stockwell's house in northern Lake Hylia, reachable after you get the Spare Key and pass through Talon and Malon's house at Lon Lon Ranch. Pour the dog food into Fifi's dish there to claim the Bottle. (If you shrink to Minish size you can even talk to Fifi, who misses his busy owner.)

quests.name: The Lost Library Books
quests.reward: Reach Librari → the Flippers and the way to the Temple of Droplets
quests.oneLine: Track down three overdue books so the librarians reshelve them, letting you reach the Minish Librari and earn the Flippers.
quests.how: Recover three checked-out books and return them so they go back on the shelf, opening the path to Librari (the Minish who once entered the Temple of Droplets): 'A Hyrulean Bestiary' (from Julietta's house, the blue-roofed place in the southeast of town — a Minish must climb in and knock the book down), 'Legend of the Picori' (from the absent-minded scholar Dr. Left's house in the southwest — shrink down, climb to the rafters, and topple it), and 'A History of Masks' (from Mayor Hagen's house near the bell in central Hyrule Town). Once all three are back, talk to Librari; after his trial he gives you the Flippers and points you toward the Temple of Droplets. This is a required story chain, but it's easy to get stuck on.

quests.name: Anju's Cucco Roundup
quests.reward: Mysterious Shells and Rupees, then a Piece of Heart
quests.oneLine: Round up escaped Cuccos against the clock over ten increasingly tricky rounds for a Heart Piece.
quests.how: Talk to Anju and accept the round-up; each of the ten rounds asks you to gather a set number of Cuccos within a time limit (it ramps from 2 birds up through fast rounds of 6–7). Pick Cuccos up and carry or toss them into the pen, and use roll/dash to cover ground. You earn Rupees and Mysterious Shells along the way, and clearing the tenth round earns a Piece of Heart. The final round needs the Flippers, since one Cucco sits across the river and must be swum to and thrown back.

quests.name: Simon's Simulation
quests.reward: Piece of Heart (first clear), then random Rupees/Kinstones/Shells
quests.oneLine: Pay to nap into a combat dream and clear the monster room for a Heart Piece.
quests.how: Simon opens his green, ghost-topped building in the central plaza after you clear the Fortress of Winds. Pay 10 Rupees to lie down and enter the simulation, then defeat every enemy in the dream room (the lineup changes with your progress in the quest). Clearing it the first time rewards a Piece of Heart; later clears give random Rupees, Kinstones, or Mysterious Shells.

quests.name: The Café Shrink Pot
quests.reward: A shortcut for shrinking to Minish size in town
quests.oneLine: Flip the café's big pot to open a Minish portal you can use to shrink down right in the middle of town.
quests.how: After you get the Cane of Pacci from the Cave of Flames, go to Mama's Café and use the cane to flip over the large pot inside. Flipping it turns the pot into a Minish portal you can hop into to become Minish-sized whenever you're in town. This makes several town fusions, chimney climbs, and the book-quest steps far quicker to reach.

quests.name: Learn the Tiger Scrolls
quests.location: Swiftblade's dojo (Hyrule Town) and dojos scattered across Hyrule
quests.reward: Tiger Scrolls — Spin Attack, Dash Attack, Rock Breaker, Down Thrust, Sword Beam, Peril Beam, Roll Attack
quests.oneLine: Visit the scattered Blade Brothers as you gain new gear to learn powerful sword techniques.
quests.how: Return to Swiftblade's dojo (southwest Hyrule Town) as you gain key items: he teaches the Spin Attack (charge and release), then the Dash Attack (Pegasus Boots), Rock Breaker (White Sword), and Down Thrust (Roc's Cape). The other brothers each live in their own spot: Grayblade (a cave on Mount Crenel) teaches the Roll Attack (swing as a roll ends), Grimblade (a cave by Hyrule Castle Garden) teaches the Sword Beam (swing at full hearts, needs the Lantern), and Waveblade (under a tree at Lake Hylia) teaches the Peril Beam (swing at one heart). Each move arrives as a Tiger Scroll, so collecting them is a tidy checklist.

quests.name: The Great Spin Attack
quests.location: Swiftblade the First's grave, Castor Wilds
quests.oneLine: Collect all seven other Tiger Scrolls and the original Swiftblade teaches the ultimate spin.
quests.how: Once you've earned the other seven Tiger Scrolls from the Blade Brothers, seek out the spirit of Swiftblade the First at his grave in Castor Wilds. He teaches the Great Spin Attack: perform a Spin Attack, then tap the button repeatedly to chain several spins together. It's the strongest melee move in the game and clears crowds fast.

quests.name: Spin & Gauge Upgrades (optional brothers)
quests.location: Their hidden dojos (reached via Kinstone fusions)
quests.reward: Faster Spin charge; faster gauge fill; longer Great Spin
quests.oneLine: Three optional Blade Brothers, reached by Kinstone fusions, refine your spin attacks rather than teaching new ones.
quests.how: After Kinstone fusions reveal them, train with Scarblade (in Castor Wilds) to make the Spin Attack charge faster, Splitblade (behind a Veil Falls waterfall) to make your gauge fill faster, and Greatblade to make the Great Spin Attack last longer. All three are optional polish on moves you already know, but they make spin-heavy fights noticeably smoother.

quests.name: Save Gregal of the Wind Tribe
quests.location: Home of the Wind Tribe, the Cloud Tops
quests.reward: 100 Mysterious Shells, later the Light Arrows
quests.oneLine: Gust-Jar the ghost haunting a bedridden Wind Tribe elder — do it in time and he upgrades your arrows.
quests.how: In the Wind Tribe's home you'll find Gregal lying in bed with a Ghost circling him. Equip the Gust Jar and suck the ghost off of him; freeing Gregal nets 100 Mysterious Shells on the spot. Return to him just before you enter the Palace of Winds and he upgrades your Arrows into Light Arrows. Important: you must save Gregal BEFORE you first reach the Cloud Tops — if you arrive without freeing him he dies, and the Light Arrows are lost for that file.

quests.name: Reach the Wind Tribe (Cloud Tops)
quests.reward: Passage to the Palace of Winds (story progress)
quests.oneLine: Find your way up to the Wind Tribe's home in the clouds, where Siroc grants passage to the final dungeon.
quests.how: Fuse Kinstones with the stranger in the western Hyrule Town house to open a portal in South Hyrule Field, step in, and you're carried up to the Cloud Tops. Speak with Siroc, the Wind Tribe's leader, and she opens the way to the tornado atop the house that whisks you to the Palace of Winds. While you're up here, save Gregal and do the Wind Tribe Kinstone fusions first — this area is easy to leave behind.

quests.name: The Tingle Siblings & the Tingle Trophy
quests.giver: Tingle, David Jr., Ankle, and Knuckle
quests.reward: Magical Boomerang (all four siblings); Tingle Trophy (all 100 fusions)
quests.oneLine: Fuse a Kinstone with each Tingle sibling for the Magical Boomerang, and finish all 100 fusions for the Tingle Trophy.
quests.how: Find and fuse a Kinstone with each of the four Tingle siblings (Tingle, David Jr., Ankle, Knuckle) scattered across Hyrule — doing all four makes trees grow in North Hyrule Field that lead to the Magical Boomerang. Separately, once you've completed all 100 Kinstone fusions in the game, Tingle converts your Kinstone Bag into the Tingle Trophy. Be aware that earning the Trophy stops Kinstone halves from dropping in the overworld, so it doubles as a 'you're done fusing' marker.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Great Mayfly Fairy
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: On an eastern ledge of the Crenel Wall (the big climbing wall on Mt. Crenel, far-right/southeast side). Look for the warning sign about throwing bombs, set a bomb to blow open the wall there, then enter and throw a bomb into her fountain. She asks if it was a golden bomb, a silver bomb, or neither, answer honestly: "Neither." (Lying makes her take all your bombs.)
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Bomb Bag upgrade, a larger bomb bag that lets Link carry up to 30 bombs.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Great Butterfly Fairy
GREAT_FAIRIES.region: Minish Woods (her tree sits in the northwest of Minish Woods, reached from the Eastern Hills)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: After getting the Cane of Pacci from the Cave of Flames, go to the far northeast corner of the Eastern Hills and use the cane on the small hole to flip up to the higher ledge, then head west into the Minish Woods to her lone tree. Throw all of your rupees into her fountain and she rewards your generosity.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Wallet upgrade, a Big Wallet. (There are three Big Wallets in the game from different sources that raise your rupee cap 99 to 300 to 500 to 999, so the exact cap after this gift depends on which others you already have.)

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Great Dragonfly Fairy
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Just inside the Royal Valley, go down the stairs and bomb the wall to the right to open a cave, then enter and approach her fountain. She asks five trivia questions, answer all correctly: No, No, Yes, No, No.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: Quiver upgrade, a larger quiver that holds up to 50 arrows. (The 99-arrow quiver comes later from a Kinstone fusion, not from this fairy.)

RUNES.name: Ezlo
RUNES.from: Story — the talking cap (a transformed Minish sage) you wear after the start; he's your map/menu helper for the whole game.
RUNES.what: The Minish Cap itself. On Minish portals he shrinks Link to tiny Picori size to enter the Minish world; once you have Roc's Cape he also lets you glide across gaps on updrafts.
RUNES.tip: Whenever you're stuck on a gap or a wall too small to climb, look for a green Minish portal (tree stump, jar, table) — Ezlo shrinks you there to find a hidden path.

RUNES.name: Smith's Sword
RUNES.from: Given by Smith (your grandfather) at home in Hyrule near the very start.
RUNES.what: Your starting sword and main attack. Swing it, and hold to charge a Spin Attack.
RUNES.tip: Hold the attack button until Link flashes, then release for a Spin Attack that hits all around you — great for crowds.

RUNES.name: White Sword
RUNES.from: Melari, the Minish smith at the top of Mt. Crenel, reforges the Broken Picori Blade into it once you've collected the first Element (Earth, from Deepwood Shrine).
RUNES.what: A stronger blade that holds the power of the Elements. As you infuse each Element at the Elemental Sanctuary in Hyrule Castle, it gains the split (clone) power and grows toward the Four Sword.
RUNES.tip: After gathering Elements, return to the Elemental Sanctuary inside Hyrule Castle to infuse them — the blade unlocks an extra clone with each step.

RUNES.name: Four Sword
RUNES.from: The White Sword infused with all four Elements (Earth, Fire, Water, Wind) at the Elemental Sanctuary.
RUNES.what: The legendary blade that seals Vaati. It's your strongest sword and reaches the full clone (split) power used to beat the final boss.
RUNES.tip: This is the goal of the whole quest — you can't truly finish Vaati without it.

RUNES.name: Sword Beam (charged shot)
RUNES.from: A power of your sword when you're at full hearts (the technique is taught by Grimblade in the Hyrule Castle garden).
RUNES.what: At full health, a normal swing fires a beam of energy that travels across the screen and hits distant enemies and switches.
RUNES.tip: Keep your hearts topped up — the free ranged beam makes fights much safer.

RUNES.name: Clone Ability (Split)
RUNES.from: Gained as you infuse Elements into the sword (one clone after the first two Elements, more with each, up to four at the Four Sword); used on glowing rune-tile pads in dungeons and the final fight.
RUNES.what: Charge the sword on a marked tile, then step onto the second tile to split into up to four Links that move in formation, letting you press several switches or strike several targets at once.
RUNES.tip: A clone vanishes if it touches a wall, enemy, or barrier (or the gauge empties) — line them up, then act fast.

RUNES.name: Mirror Shield
RUNES.from: Post-game only — after you beat Vaati and finish the Goron Kinstone quest, give your shield to Biggoron at Veil Falls and return later to receive it. (You start the game with a Small Shield that Zelda wins for you at the Picori Festival.)
RUNES.what: Blocks attacks like the normal shield, and also reflects projectiles and beams back at the enemy.
RUNES.tip: Hold it up against magic shots and beams to bounce them back instead of just blocking.

RUNES.name: Gust Jar
RUNES.from: Deepwood Shrine (Dungeon 1), in the chest after you beat the Madderpillar mini-boss.
RUNES.what: Hold the button to suck in air — it pulls items, mushrooms, and light enemies toward you, clears sand/cobwebs, and tugs you along lilypads. Release for a puff of air.
RUNES.tip: It's also a tool against enemies and ghosts: vacuum up small spinning foes and dust piles, and use it to topple the Big Green ChuChu boss.

RUNES.name: Cane of Pacci
RUNES.from: Cave of Flames (Dungeon 2).
RUNES.what: Flips objects and enemies over with a magic ring, and turns holes into launch pads — stand on a flipped hole to be shot upward to a higher ledge.
RUNES.tip: Flip an armored enemy onto its back to expose it, then strike. Use it on dark holes to reach ledges you can't jump to.

RUNES.name: Mole Mitts
RUNES.from: Fortress of Winds (Dungeon 3).
RUNES.what: Claw gloves that dig through soft dirt walls and floors, opening new tunnels and uncovering buried paths.
RUNES.tip: Any crumbly brown wall or dirt patch can be dug — look for them to find shortcuts and hidden rooms (and the Goron-quest caves).

RUNES.name: Flame Lantern
RUNES.from: Temple of Droplets (Dungeon 4).
RUNES.what: Shoots a small flame to light torches, burn obstacles, and melt hunks of ice — and the light lets you see in dark rooms.
RUNES.tip: Use it to thaw the frozen blocks and enemies in the icy temple, and to light far-off torches you can't reach by hand.

RUNES.name: Roc's Cape
RUNES.from: Palace of Winds (Dungeon 5).
RUNES.what: Lets Link jump and briefly hover/glide, crossing wide gaps and floating over spikes and pits.
RUNES.tip: Tap to jump short gaps; hold to glide a little farther. Combine the jump with a downward sword stab for extra reach.

RUNES.name: Bow
RUNES.from: Found in northwest Castor Wilds (reachable only at Minish size).
RUNES.what: Fires arrows at distant enemies, switches, and eye targets. Your main ranged weapon.
RUNES.tip: Arrows are limited — pick up dropped and grass-cut arrows, and save the Bow for switches you can't reach with the sword beam.

RUNES.name: Light Arrow
RUNES.from: Fuse Kinstones with Strato in western Hyrule Town to open a portal in South Hyrule Field to the Wind Tribe's home; there, use the Gust Jar to drive the ghost off the bedridden old man Gregal — do it before reaching the Wind Tribe via Veil Falls. A side reward, not a dungeon item.
RUNES.what: Upgrades your arrows: hold an arrow to charge a glowing Light Arrow that deals extra damage, especially to dark/evil foes (uncharged, it fires as a normal arrow).
RUNES.tip: Optional and missable — save Gregal in time or the Light Arrow is gone for that file.

RUNES.name: Boomerang
RUNES.from: Bought from Stockwell's shop in Hyrule Town (300 Rupees).
RUNES.what: Throws and returns, stunning enemies and grabbing distant items, switches, and levers.
RUNES.tip: Great for hitting far switches and snagging items over gaps without crossing — it also briefly freezes many enemies.

RUNES.name: Magical Boomerang
RUNES.from: Fuse Kinstones with all four Tingle siblings, which opens four trees in North Hyrule Field; press the switches in them, then take the chest from the central tree.
RUNES.what: A steerable, longer-range boomerang — you can curve its flight to reach awkward switches and items.
RUNES.tip: Guide it with the d-pad mid-flight, so aim around walls to hit things a straight throw would miss.

RUNES.name: Bombs
RUNES.from: The Bomb Bag is given by a Picori in a house near Hyrule Town after you clear Deepwood Shrine.
RUNES.what: Throw or set bombs to blow open cracked walls, damage enemies, and hit switches. Carried in the Bomb Bag.
RUNES.tip: Watch walls and floors for thin cracks — bomb them to find hidden rooms full of Rupees and Kinstones.

RUNES.name: Remote Bombs
RUNES.from: Fuse Kinstones with Elder Gentari in Minish Village, then visit Belari to unlock them.
RUNES.what: A second bomb type you can detonate on command — set one, then press the button again to blow it exactly when you want.
RUNES.tip: Toggle between normal and Remote Bombs in the menu; Remote Bombs are perfect for timing a blast as an enemy walks past.

RUNES.name: Pegasus Boots
RUNES.from: Buy the Wake-Up Mushroom from Syrup the witch (60 Rupees), then use it on Rem the sleeping shoemaker in Hyrule Town — he gives you the Boots he was making.
RUNES.what: Hold the button to charge, then dash forward fast — smash into cracked blocks/trees, stun enemies, and cross slippery or timed gaps.
RUNES.tip: Dashing into certain trees and posts shakes loose Rupees, hearts, and Mysterious Shells; the boots also let you cross Castor Wilds.

RUNES.name: Grip Ring
RUNES.from: Bought from a Business Scrub in a bomb-opened cave on Mt. Crenel (40 Rupees).
RUNES.what: Lets Link cling to and climb rocky walls without being blown or knocked off by the wind.
RUNES.tip: You need it to scale the windy cliffs of Mt. Crenel toward the Cave of Flames — buy it before trying to climb.

RUNES.name: Flippers
RUNES.from: Return the three overdue library books to the Hyrule Town library, which opens the way to Librari; clear his short trial to receive the Flippers.
RUNES.what: Let Link swim and dive across deep water instead of being blocked at the edge.
RUNES.tip: Once you can swim, revisit earlier areas — plenty of islands and underwater paths were unreachable before.

RUNES.name: Bottle (Empty Bottle)
RUNES.from: Four total around Hyrule: a Business Scrub in Trilby Highlands (~20 Rupees), Stockwell's shop (free after feeding his dog at Lake Hylia), Eastern Hills (Kinstone-fuse with Smith, your grandfather), and Lon Lon Ranch's Goron Cave (after the Goron Kinstone quest).
RUNES.what: Stores fairies, potions, water, milk, Picolyte, and small creatures. A bottled fairy auto-revives you when you fall.
RUNES.tip: Always keep at least one fairy in a bottle before a dungeon or boss — it's a free second life.

RUNES.name: Red Potion
RUNES.from: Bought from the witch Syrup's shop and stored in a Bottle.
RUNES.what: Drink to refill several hearts of health.
RUNES.tip: Buy and bottle one before tough fights — it's faster than hunting for hearts mid-battle.

RUNES.name: White Picolyte
RUNES.from: Bought at the Hyrule Town potion shop (available early), stored in a Bottle.
RUNES.what: Temporarily raises your chance of finding Kinstone pieces from grass, pots, and enemies.
RUNES.tip: Drink it before sweeping an area you're farming for Kinstones to fill your bag faster.

RUNES.name: Blue Picolyte
RUNES.from: Unlocked at the Hyrule Town potion shop once the matching Pico Bloom is grown, stored in a Bottle.
RUNES.what: Temporarily raises your chance of finding item drops (hearts, arrows, bombs) from enemies and grass.
RUNES.tip: Pair it with a clearing run when you want consumables to drop more often.

RUNES.name: Green Picolyte
RUNES.from: Unlocked at the Hyrule Town potion shop once the matching Pico Bloom is grown, stored in a Bottle.
RUNES.what: Temporarily raises your chance of finding Mysterious Shells.
RUNES.tip: Drink it before farming Shells so you can afford Carlov's figurines sooner.

RUNES.name: Yellow Picolyte
RUNES.from: Unlocked at the Hyrule Town potion shop once the matching Pico Bloom is grown, stored in a Bottle.
RUNES.what: Temporarily raises your chance of finding Rupees.
RUNES.tip: Handy when you're saving up for the Boomerang or a wallet/quiver upgrade.

RUNES.name: Kinstone Bag
RUNES.from: Given early in the story as you learn to fuse Kinstones.
RUNES.what: Holds the Kinstone halves you collect. Match a half with an NPC or creature who has the other half to 'fuse' and trigger an event somewhere in Hyrule (chests appear, paths open, plants grow).
RUNES.tip: Talk to everyone and fuse whenever the icon appears — many fusions open shortcuts and reward chests you'd otherwise never reach.

RUNES.name: Mysterious Shell
RUNES.from: Found all over Hyrule — in grass, pots, dig spots, and from dashing into trees.
RUNES.what: The figurine currency. Trade Shells to Carlov in Hyrule Town to roll for collectible figurines of the game's characters.
RUNES.tip: Pick up every Shell you see; figurine-hunting is a long optional collection, and Green Picolyte makes Shells drop more.

RUNES.name: Figurines
RUNES.from: Won from Carlov's figurine machine by spending Mysterious Shells.
RUNES.what: Collectible statuettes of nearly every character and enemy; collecting them all is the game's big optional completion goal and unlocks a few small rewards.
RUNES.tip: Purely optional — chase these only if you enjoy 100% completion, since it takes a lot of Shells.

RUNES.name: Bomb Bag Upgrade
RUNES.from: Three upgrades around Hyrule (the Mt. Crenel Great Fairy, an Elder Gentari Kinstone fusion, and Stockwell's shop), collected in any order.
RUNES.what: Each raises how many Bombs you can carry — to 30, then 50, then 99.
RUNES.tip: More bombs means less backtracking to refill; grab these as they open up.

RUNES.name: Big Quiver
RUNES.from: Three upgrades: a Kinstone fusion with Strato, Stockwell's shop (600 Rupees), and the Great Butterfly Fairy in Royal Valley.
RUNES.what: Raises how many arrows you can hold — to 50, then 70, then 99.
RUNES.tip: Worth it once you rely on the Bow — a bigger quiver saves trips back for arrows.

RUNES.name: Big Wallet
RUNES.from: Three upgrades: Stockwell's shop (80 Rupees), the Great Fairy in Eastern Hills, and a Lon Lon Ranch reward (the base wallet holds 100).
RUNES.what: Raises your Rupee carrying limit — to 300, then 500, then 999.
RUNES.tip: Grab the first wallet upgrade early so you stop wasting Rupees when chests overfill your purse.

CHAMPIONS.name: Earth Element
CHAMPIONS.note: The first of the four Elements, won from the Deepwood Shrine deep in the Minish Woods.

CHAMPIONS.name: Fire Element
CHAMPIONS.note: The second Element, won from the Cave of Flames inside Mount Crenel.

CHAMPIONS.name: Water Element
CHAMPIONS.note: The third Element, won from the frozen Temple of Droplets beneath Lake Hylia.

CHAMPIONS.name: Wind Element
CHAMPIONS.note: The fourth and last Element, won from the Palace of Winds high in the Cloud Tops — completing the Four Sword.

ROADMAP.name: The Four Elements
ROADMAP.note: Earth, Fire, Water, and Wind — won from the Deepwood Shrine, the Cave of Flames, the Temple of Droplets, and the Palace of Winds. Infuse them into the blade at the Elemental Sanctuary to forge the Four Sword.

ROADMAP.name: Kinstone Fusions
ROADMAP.note: Carry Kinstone halves and fuse them with townsfolk and creatures (hold the right one and a green beam links you). The 100 fusions open chests, bridges, Heart Pieces, and secret caves all over Hyrule.

ROADMAP.name: Figurine Collection
ROADMAP.note: Spend the Mysterious Shells you gather (and vacuum up with the Gust Jar) on the figurine gachapon to complete the gallery of every character and enemy.

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: Forty-four are hidden across Hyrule — many behind Kinstone fusions or only reachable while Minish-sized.

TIPS.name: Shrinking with Ezlo
items: Wear Ezlo and step onto a Minish Portal (glowing stumps, jars, holes) to shrink to the size of the tiny Picori. At that scale, puddles are lakes and a book is a building — whole hidden routes open up.
Minish-sized, you can slip through cracks and grates, climb tiny ladders, and talk to the Minish, who give hints and help. Many puzzles need you to shrink, cross, then return to normal size.
Ezlo also narrates and nudges — if you're lost, he often hints at the next step.

TIPS.name: Kinstone fusion
items: Collect Kinstone halves (green, blue, red, gold) from grass, chests, and enemies. When an NPC or creature has a glowing thought-bubble, talk to them and FUSE a matching half — a green beam links your worlds and triggers an event somewhere in Hyrule.
Fusions open chests, raise bridges, sprout beanstalks, and reveal Heart Pieces. There are 100 in all — fuse with everyone you meet.
Gold Kinstones are rare and tied to specific big rewards; keep an eye out for who wants them.

TIPS.name: Reforging the blade
items: Vaati shattered the Picori Blade. You collect the four Elements and infuse them into the sword at the Elemental Sanctuary, upgrading it (White Sword → Four Sword) along the way.
The finished Four Sword can split Link into copies — stand on the marked tiles and hold the sword to spawn clones for block and switch puzzles.
Picolyte (bought in town) and bottled fairies help you survive the tougher dungeons.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Pieces of Heart
COLLECTIBLES.note: Every 4 make a full Heart Container — many hidden behind Kinstone fusions or only reachable while Minish-sized.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Kinstone Fusions
COLLECTIBLES.note: Match Kinstone halves with townsfolk and creatures to trigger 100 events across Hyrule.
